turns.content: What brings you in today?

I've had a sore throat and a persistent cough for about three days now. It started with a scratchy feeling and has progressed to a dry cough that's worse at night. My roommate was diagnosed with the flu last week, so I thought it would be prudent to come in and make sure I don't need any treatment, even though I feel like it's just a minor virus.
When did this start?

The sore throat started about three days ago, on Monday evening, and the cough developed the following day. It's been pretty consistent since then, though the coughing fits seem to increase at night and when I first wake up.
Can you describe the pain or discomfort? Where exactly do you feel it?

It's mostly a raw, scratchy sensation in my throat, specifically around the back of my throat near the tonsils. Swallowing makes it slightly worse, but it's not sharp or stabbing — more of a dull ache. I haven't noticed any chest pain or body aches, just that localized discomfort.
Have you had any fever or chills?

I've been checking my temperature periodically, and it's been consistently normal, around 98.6°F. No chills or night sweats either. However, I did feel a bit more tired than usual yesterday, and my voice has been slightly hoarse today.
What medications are you currently taking?

I'm not on any prescription medications right now. I did take some over-the-counter throat lozenges and one dose of ibuprofen yesterday for the discomfort, but I haven't taken anything else. No antibiotics or cold medicine.
Do you have any chronic medical conditions?

I do have mild asthma, but it's usually well-controlled with just an albuterol inhaler as needed, and I haven't needed it much recently. I also have seasonal pollen allergies, but I'm not on any daily medication for that either.
Are you allergic to any medications?
No, I don't have any known allergies to medications. I've had penicillin and other common antibiotics before without any issues.
Have you tried anything to relieve the symptoms?

Besides the ibuprofen and lozenges, I've been drinking warm water with honey and using a humidifier in my room at night. I also tried a saline gargle once, but it didn't seem to make much difference. My asthma inhaler didn't help the cough either, which made me think it's likely not related to my asthma.
Does anything make it better or worse?

Lying flat tends to make the cough worse, so propping myself up with pillows helps a bit. Talking for a while also seems to aggravate the sore throat, which is why my voice is getting a little hoarse. Warm liquids like tea or honey water provide temporary relief for the throat discomfort, but the coughing fits return once the effect wears off.
How has this been affecting your daily life?

It's been a bit disruptive, honestly. I'm a university student, so I've had to miss a couple of lectures because the coughing fits were too distracting, and I don't want to disrupt others. My sleep has also been lighter than usual due to the nighttime coughing, so I've been feeling more fatigued during the day. But I'm still able to study and get my work done — it's just taking a little more effort.
Do you smoke or drink alcohol?

No, I don't smoke at all — never have. I do drink occasionally, maybe a glass of wine or a cocktail with friends once or twice a month, but not regularly and not recently since I've been feeling under the weather.
Has anyone in your family had similar problems?

No one in my immediate family has had a sore throat or cough recently. But as I mentioned, my roommate was definitely sick with flu-like symptoms about a week before I started feeling this — she had a high fever and body aches, which I haven't experienced. My mother does tend to get sinus infections easily, but I don't think she's had one recently.
Have you experienced anything like this before?

Yes, I've had a few flus and colds before, especially during winter terms in college, but nothing that felt exactly like this. Usually my allergies cause more congestion and sneezing, whereas this time it's primarily the sore throat and dry cough without much nasal involvement. So it feels a bit different from my typical seasonal episodes.
On a scale of 1 to 10, how would you rate what you're going through right now — physically, and also emotionally?

Physically, I'd say it's mild — maybe a 2 out of 10. The sore throat and cough are more annoying than truly painful, and I can still function through my day. Emotionally, I'm about a 2 as well — I'm not worried or anxious, just a bit tired and slightly frustrated that it's keeping me from my usual routine and concentration. I'm confident it's nothing serious, but I wanted a professional opinion to be safe.
Is there anything else you think I should know?

Actually, I did notice a slight change in my sense of taste this morning — everything tastes a bit muted, but that might be from the throat inflammation. Also, I haven't had any shortness of breath or wheezing, which I'm relieved about given my asthma history. And I suppose I should mention that I did try my inhaler once, but it had no effect on the cough, which I found interesting. Other than that, I think that covers everything.
And how have you been coping with all of this?

I've been trying to take it easy — I've reduced my caffeine intake, made sure to stay hydrated, and I've been going to bed earlier even if sleep is interrupted. I also let my professors know, which helped reduce some pressure. Overall, I'm managing okay; I'm not panicking, just taking it one day at a time and hoping rest and fluids will do the trick. I mainly wanted to come in to make sure it's not something that needs more specific treatment, like antibiotics.
And how have you been coping with all of this?

I've been trying to stay positive and not let it derail my routine too much. I'm resting more, drinking plenty of fluids, and using my humidifier religiously. I've also been wearing a mask when I go out to avoid spreading anything, just in case. Honestly, it's manageable — I'm just hoping it resolves on its own soon so I can get back to normal.
And how have you been coping with all of this?

I think I've been handling it fairly well. I'm trying to balance rest with my responsibilities, so I'm not pushing myself too hard. I make sure to take breaks between studying, I gargle with warm salt water a few times a day, and I'm avoiding caffeine in the evening to help with sleep. It's not ideal, but I'm coping without too much stress.
And how have you been coping with all of this?

I've been leaning on my roommate a bit for support since she just recovered from the flu, so she understands what I'm going through. She's been making sure I eat well and reminding me to rest. I also set up a little "recovery station" by my bed with water, tissues, and lozenges, so I don't have to get up as often. It's working well enough, though I'm looking forward to feeling back to normal soon.
And how have you been coping with all of this?

I think I've been handling it pretty well, all things considered. I’m staying hydrated, using my humidifier, and trying to rest when I can. My roommate has been supportive, and I’ve kept up with my schoolwork in shorter bursts, so I feel like I’m managing without too much disruption. It’s just annoying more than anything.
